story.asA: photographer or developer validating Recipe's conversion accuracy
story.iWant: to compare parameters between two preset files (even across different formats)
story.soThat: I can verify that conversions preserve color adjustments correctly and identify any parameter drift or data loss
story.tasks:
### Task 1: Create Diff Types and Core Logic (AC-1, AC-2) - [ ] 1.1 Create internal/inspect/diff.go file - [ ] 1.2 Define DiffResult type - [ ] 1.3 Implement Diff() function - [ ] 1.4 Handle different data types - [ ] 1.5 Add unit tests
### Task 2: Create CLI Command (AC-1, AC-3, AC-4, AC-5, AC-6) - [ ] 2.1 Create cmd/cli/diff.go file - [ ] 2.2 Define diffCmd Cobra command - [ ] 2.3 Add command flags - [ ] 2.4 Implement runDiff() function - [ ] 2.5 Implement helper functions - [ ] 2.6 Add CLI integration tests
### Task 3: Implement Output Formatting (AC-3, AC-4, AC-5) - [ ] 3.1 Implement formatDiff() - [ ] 3.2 Implement formatDiffJSON() - [ ] 3.3 Implement colorizeOutput() - [ ] 3.4 Implement terminal detection - [ ] 3.5 Add formatting tests
### Task 4: Significant Change Detection (AC-2) - [ ] 4.1 Implement significance calculation in Diff() - [ ] 4.2 Add tolerance parameter for float comparisons - [ ] 4.3 Test significance detection
### Task 5: Cross-Format Diff Support (AC-1) - [ ] 5.1 Ensure Diff() operates on UniversalRecipe - [ ] 5.2 Test cross-format diffs - [ ] 5.3 Handle format-specific fields - [ ] 5.4 Add round-trip diff tests
### Task 6: Performance Optimization and Benchmarking (AC-7) - [ ] 6.1 Create diff_test.go file - [ ] 6.2 Implement benchmark tests - [ ] 6.3 Run benchmarks and validate <100ms target - [ ] 6.4 Optimize if needed - [ ] 6.5 Profile memory usage
### Task 7: Documentation and Integration - [ ] 7.1 Update README with diff tool examples - [ ] 7.2 Update help text - [ ] 7.3 Add to Makefile (if needed) - [ ] 7.4 Create example diff output for documentation - [ ] 7.5 Add to CI/CD validation

story-context.acceptanceCriteria:
### AC-1: Parameter Comparison Across Formats - recipe diff FILE1 FILE2 compares all parameters between two preset files - Works with any format combination (NP3 vs NP3, NP3 vs XMP, XMP vs lrtemplate, etc.) - Automatically detects both file formats (no explicit format flags required) - Shows added, removed, and modified parameters in clear human-readable format - Handles files from different formats by comparing via UniversalRecipe intermediate representation
### AC-2: Significant Change Detection - Diff highlights "significant" changes (>5% for numeric values) - Significant changes marked with *significant indicator or color - Minor changes (<5%) shown but not highlighted - Tolerance configurable via --tolerance flag (default: 0.001 for floats) - Helps users focus on meaningful differences vs. rounding noise
### AC-3: Unified Mode (Show All Fields) - --unified flag shows all fields, not just changes - Unchanged fields displayed as: FieldName: value = value - Changed fields displayed as: FieldName: old_value → new_value - Helps users verify complete parameter set, not just differences
### AC-4: JSON Output for Automation - --format=json outputs diff results as structured JSON - JSON contains array of DiffResult objects - Each result has: field, old_value, new_value, change_type, significant - Enables automated validation scripts and CI/CD integration - JSON is valid and parseable by standard tools (jq, Python json module)
### AC-5: Color-Coded Terminal Output - Changed parameters highlighted in color (if terminal supports ANSI) - Added parameters shown in green - Removed parameters shown in red - Significant changes shown in bold or bright color - --no-color flag disables coloring for piping/redirects - Auto-detects terminal capabilities (no color in pipes)
### AC-6: Error Handling - Clear error messages for unsupported file formats - Handles corrupted files gracefully (reports parsing errors) - Validates both files exist before comparison - Returns meaningful exit codes (0=no diff, 1=differences found, 2=error) - Suggests fixes for common errors
### AC-7: Performance Requirement - Diff completes in <100ms for typical preset files - Two parses + comparison + formatting within time budget - Performance matches single conversion goal (<100ms Epic 1) - Benchmark tests validate performance target - No performance regressions from Epic 1 parser speed

doc.title: Product Requirements Document
doc.snippet: Compare parameters between files using recipe diff FILE1 FILE2 command. Shows added/removed/changed parameters, highlights significant differences, works across different formats. Acceptance criteria includes showing changes, highlighting significance (>5%), supporting cross-format comparison.

doc.title: Epic 5 Technical Specification
doc.snippet: Epic 5 adds inspection tools to Recipe CLI. Diff tool compares UniversalRecipe structs field-by-field using reflection, operates on parsed output from Epic 1 parsers, returns formatted string with changed parameters only. Performance target: <100ms for two parses + comparison + formatting.

doc.title: Architecture Documentation
doc.snippet: UniversalRecipe serves as central hub for all conversions. All parsers convert Format → UniversalRecipe. Diff tool leverages this by comparing two UniversalRecipe instances, making cross-format diff transparent - Epic 1 parsers handle parameter mapping, diff just compares normalized representations.

doc.title: Architecture Documentation
doc.snippet: Commands use cobra.Command with Use, Short, Long, Args (ExactArgs), and RunE. Flags use StringP, Bool, Float64 patterns. Error handling returns errors to Cobra for exit code 1. Diff command follows same structure as inspect and convert commands.

doc.title: Epic 5 Technical Specification
doc.snippet: DiffResult struct contains: Field (string), OldValue (interface{}), NewValue (interface{}), ChangeType (string: modified/added/removed), Significant (bool: >5% change). Used by Diff() function and formatters.

artifact.reason: Core data model that diff command compares. Contains 50+ fields with JSON/XML tags. Diff operates on two instances of this struct using reflection to iterate over all fields.
Root command where diff command will be registered. Shows global flags (--verbose, --json), version handling, and logger initialization patterns to follow.
Example CLI command structure to follow for diff command. Shows Cobra pattern, flag handling, detectFormat usage, error handling, and logging patterns.
NP3 parser that returns *models.UniversalRecipe. Reused by diff command to parse NP3 files before comparison. No modifications needed.
XMP parser that returns *models.UniversalRecipe. Reused by diff command to parse XMP files before comparison. No modifications needed.
Lrtemplate parser that returns *models.UniversalRecipe. Reused by diff command to parse lrtemplate files before comparison. No modifications needed.
Format detection function that diff command uses to identify file types before parsing. Returns "np3", "xmp", or "lrtemplate" based on file content and extension.
Error type used across Epic 1 for format-specific errors. Diff command should use same pattern for consistency: wrap parse errors with context about which file failed.

story-context.constraints:
### CRITICAL Constraints - MUST use reflection to iterate over UniversalRecipe fields (avoid manual field comparison) - MUST compare via UniversalRecipe intermediate (do NOT implement format-specific diff logic) - MUST reuse Epic 1 parsers without modification (np3.Parse, xmp.Parse, lrtemplate.Parse) - MUST follow Unix diff exit code convention (0=no diff, 1=diff found, 2=error) - MUST output valid JSON when --format=json flag used - MUST detect terminal capabilities and disable color when piped/redirected - MUST complete in <100ms for typical preset files (two parses + comparison + formatting)
### Development Constraints - NO modifications to Epic 1 parsers or converters (purely additive) - NO modifications to internal/model/recipe.go (read-only operations) - NO framework dependencies beyond Cobra (already in use from Epic 3) - NO new data structures for UniversalRecipe (use existing struct as-is)
### Performance Constraints - Reflection overhead must be <5ms (acceptable within 100ms budget) - Memory usage <20 MB (two UniversalRecipe structs in memory) - Minimize allocations in hot loop (field comparison) - Use strings.Builder for output formatting (avoid repeated concatenation)
### Code Quality Constraints - Follow Epic 3 CLI command pattern (Cobra structure, flag handling, error wrapping) - Use slog for structured logging (same as Epic 3) - Table-driven tests for diff logic validation - Benchmark tests to validate <100ms target

interface.name: Diff
interface.signature: func Diff(recipe1, recipe2 *models.UniversalRecipe, tolerance float64) ([]DiffResult, error)
interface.usage: Core diff logic. Compares two UniversalRecipe structs field-by-field using reflection. Returns slice of DiffResult structs with change details.

interface.name: FormatDiff
interface.signature: func FormatDiff(results []DiffResult, unified, colorize bool) string
interface.usage: Formats diff results as human-readable text. Groups by change type (modified, added, removed, unchanged). Applies ANSI color codes if colorize=true.

interface.name: FormatDiffJSON
interface.signature: func FormatDiffJSON(results []DiffResult, file1, file2 string) ([]byte, error)
interface.usage: Formats diff results as structured JSON for automation. Returns marshaled JSON with file names, change counts, and diff array.

interface.name: DiffResult
interface.signature: type DiffResult struct { Field string, OldValue interface{}, NewValue interface{}, ChangeType string, Significant bool }
interface.usage: Represents a single field difference. ChangeType is "modified", "added", or "removed". Significant is true if change >5%.

interface.name: np3.Parse (existing)
interface.usage: Reused by diff command to parse NP3 files. No modifications needed.

interface.name: xmp.Parse (existing)
interface.usage: Reused by diff command to parse XMP files. No modifications needed.

interface.name: lrtemplate.Parse (existing)
interface.usage: Reused by diff command to parse lrtemplate files. No modifications needed.

interface.name: detectFormat (existing)
interface.usage: Reused by diff command to detect file formats. Returns "np3", "xmp", or "lrtemplate".

tests.standards: - Use table-driven tests for diff logic (various field combinations) - Test cross-format diffs (NP3 vs XMP, XMP vs lrtemplate, etc.) - Test significance calculation (>5% vs <5% changes) - Test tolerance parameter for float comparisons - Test all output formats (text, JSON, unified, colored) - Benchmark tests to validate <100ms target - Integration tests for CLI command end-to-end - Test error handling (missing files, invalid formats, parse errors)
tests.locations: - internal/inspect/diff_test.go (unit tests and benchmarks) - cmd/cli/diff_test.go (integration tests for CLI command)
tests.ideas: - TestDiff_DetectsChanges: Verify modified/added/removed detection - TestDiff_CrossFormat: NP3 vs XMP comparison correctness - TestDiff_SignificantChanges: >5% threshold marking - TestDiff_Tolerance: Float comparison tolerance handling - TestDiff_UnifiedMode: All fields shown in output - TestFormatDiff_Text: Human-readable text formatting - TestFormatDiff_JSON: Valid JSON output structure - TestDiff_ColorCodes: ANSI color code application - TestDiff_TerminalDetection: Auto-disable color when piped - TestDiff_ErrorHandling: Missing file, invalid format, parse error cases - TestDiff_ExitCodes: 0=no diff, 1=diff found, 2=error - BenchmarkDiff: Validate <100ms total time (read + parse + compare + format) - BenchmarkDiff_CompareOnly: Measure comparison logic overhead (~5ms target) - TestDiff_RoundTrip: NP3→XMP→NP3 should show minimal differences
